headline: CUT YOUR ENERGY BILLS BY UP TO 30%
prompt_text:
You are being given six attached images for this composition:

IMAGE 1 (reference ad — A1 structural template): This is the MANDATORY structural template for this archetype. Mirror its layout exactly — left-half text block with a stacked headline anchored top-left, an accent-coloured pill embedded mid-headline carrying a connector phrase, a price block beneath the headline, a dark horizontal footer strip running edge-to-edge across the bottom containing trust pills separated by thin vertical dividers, a thin brand-logo strip at the very bottom edge, the hero house diagram occupying the right half and bleeding off the right canvas edge with a coloured geometric accent shape behind it, and a circular tilted starburst discount/guarantee badge sitting in the lower-right corner overlapping the footer strip. Density, hierarchy, and zone geometry are LOCKED to this reference. DO NOT copy its exact palette, exact headline wording, exact price, or exact pill text — those come from the brief below.

IMAGE 2 (gold-standard A1 output — quality bar only): Use this only as calibration for typographic polish, badge confidence, tick-bullet refinement, and overall production finish. DO NOT copy its layout — Image 2 uses a different structure. Image 1 is the structural template; Image 2 is the quality bar.

IMAGE 3 (locked component — house_3d_blue): Use this house diagram AS-IS as the right-half hero. Do NOT redraw, do NOT regenerate the ducting, do NOT modify the silhouette, the airflow arrows, the rooftop condenser unit, the windows, or the proportions. You may apply only a subtle palette tint (5–10% overlay) to integrate it with the chosen palette — the structure must remain identical to the source. HR02 and HR15 forbid generating diagrams from scratch.

IMAGES 4, 5, 6 (locked brand logos — Daikin, Mitsubishi Electric, Samsung): Use these AS-IS in their native brand colours along the bottom brand strip. Do NOT recolour, do NOT redraw, do NOT distort proportions. Place them evenly spaced.

Create a square 1080×1080 social media ad for Sharp Air Conditioning, a residential ducted air conditioning installer servicing the Central Coast, Newcastle and Hunter Valley regions of NSW, Australia.

FUNNEL CONTEXT
This ad targets a cold/warm audience considering ducted air conditioning as a long-term home upgrade. The job is calm, authoritative, savings-led — ducted A/C as the obvious financial decision that pays itself back through reduced energy bills. Not pain framing. Not urgency framing. The mood is confident, modern, benefit-led — the smart-money choice.

CRITICAL CLARITY RULE
A scroller must understand within ONE SECOND that this ad is for DUCTED AIR CONDITIONING with a strong savings benefit. The phrase "DUCTED A/C" (or "DUCTED AIR CONDITIONING") MUST appear prominently in the headline zone AND be reinforced in the price pill — not buried, not shrunk to footnote scale. Clarity beats cleverness. Premium aesthetic does not override this.

PALETTE & MOOD — pick fresh per generation
Calm, trustworthy, savings-led. Pick a fresh modern palette per generation — clean, confident, premium-modern. Suggested mood territory: cool sophisticated tones (deep teal/charcoal/cream, modern navy/sky-blue/white, sage/cream/charcoal, or forest-green/cream/charcoal). NOT aggressive reds. NOT moody darks. NOT the reference ad's exact grey/blue. NOT the gold-standard's exact green. One strong accent colour reserved for the savings numeral, the embedded headline pill, the CTA, and the badge — the same accent colour ties them together. Typography: pair a confident bold sans-serif for the headline with a clean medium-weight sans for the body. Generous breathing room. Should feel like a modern home-brand ad, not a discount-retailer flyer.

LAYOUT — mirrors the reference ad structure (Image 1)

LEFT HALF — text-led on a clean light/cream background

1. TOP REGION HEADER — small, sitting just inside the safe zone, top-left
Tiny medium-weight uppercase caps with dot separators: "CENTRAL COAST · NEWCASTLE · HUNTER VALLEY"
Set in the deep palette colour. Subtle but legible at thumbnail — anchors the local-trust signal without competing with the headline.

2. HEADLINE — upper-left, dominant element of the ad
Stacked headline reading exactly: "CUT YOUR ENERGY BILLS BY UP TO 30%"
Built across four lines with rhythmic weight contrast (mirrors the reference ad):
   - Line 1: "CUT YOUR" — medium-weight uppercase, smaller scale, set in the deep palette colour
   - Line 2: "ENERGY BILLS" — oversized bold uppercase, the visual anchor of the headline, set in the deep palette colour
   - Line 3: "BY UP TO" — small uppercase white text sitting INSIDE a horizontal accent-coloured pill (this pill is the visual signature element, mirroring the reference's blue "BY UP TO" pill)
   - Line 4: "30%" — oversized bold in the contrasting accent colour, dominant numeral, the savings hit
Headline occupies roughly the upper 45% of the left zone with confident scale.

3. SUB-LINE — directly beneath the headline
Medium-weight, clearly readable: "On Your Whole-Home DUCTED A/C"
"DUCTED A/C" set in the deep palette colour and bolded — this reinforces clarity directly under the headline so the product category is unmissable.

4. PRICE BLOCK — beneath the sub-line, left side, mid-canvas
Mirrors the reference ad's price-block treatment:
   - Small medium-weight: "Ducted A/C Installed from"
   - Oversized bold dominant numeral: "$57"
   - Beside the numeral, smaller, stacked: "Per Week"
The price block is a confident statement, not a footnote. Sized to read at thumbnail.

RIGHT HALF — hero visual

5. HOUSE DIAGRAM HERO
Place the attached house_3d_blue diagram (IMAGE 3) as the right-half hero, occupying roughly 50–55% of the canvas width. The diagram bleeds off the right canvas edge for cinematic depth (mirrors the reference). Position vertically centred against the headline. Use AS-IS — same silhouette, ducting, arrows, condenser. Behind the diagram on the right side, place a soft accent-coloured geometric wedge or angled triangle (mirrors the reference's accent-shape treatment) — adds depth and brand-ad polish. Subtle palette tint may be applied to the diagram for cohesion; structure remains identical.

DARK FOOTER STRIP — running full canvas width, ~14–16% of canvas height

6. TRUST PILLS — three short callouts evenly spaced inside the dark strip, each separated by a thin vertical accent-coloured divider line (mirrors reference). Text in white/cream:
   - "Save up to 30% on bills"
   - "$1,299 in FREE Bonuses"
   - "$0 Upfront Cost"
Each pill clearly readable at thumbnail size — meaningful element, NOT decorative footnote.

7. CTA BUTTON — bottom-left, sitting integrated with or just above the footer strip
Rounded pill button reading: "BOOK A FREE ON-SITE QUOTE"
Bold uppercase white text on the accent-colour fill. Confident sizing — pops as the action element.

8. CIRCULAR DISCOUNT/GUARANTEE BADGE — bottom-right corner, overlapping the footer strip, slightly tilted 5–10°
Circular starburst/seal in the accent colour, white or cream text inside, sized roughly 18–22% of canvas width:
   - Top arc: "5-YEAR"
   - Bottom arc: "GUARANTEE"
Mirrors the reference ad's badge placement and treatment.

9. TRUST BADGES — small accent pills, lower-left zone, just above the footer strip
Two small circular seal badges sitting side by side (each ~8–10% canvas width), styled in the deep palette colour with cream/white iconography, generated fresh in the brand's visual style:
   - "FAMILY OWNED & OPERATED" — small house-with-heart icon
   - "AUSTRALIAN OWNED" — small map-of-Australia icon
Per HR19, generate these in a style that matches the ad's palette. Subtle but legible — they sit as supporting trust accents, not competing with the dominant headline or guarantee badge.

VERY BOTTOM EDGE — brand logo strip

10. BRAND LOGOS
Thin horizontal strip across the very bottom on a light/cream band, with three brand logos evenly spaced and rendered in their native colours: Daikin (IMAGE 4), Mitsubishi Electric (IMAGE 5), Samsung (IMAGE 6). Use the locked logos AS PROVIDED — do not recolour, do not redraw, do not resize disproportionately.

PRIORITY HIERARCHY (highest to lowest)
- ARCHETYPE-SPECIFIC RULES dominate: calm savings-led mood, never pain framing, per-week price only (never fixed), house diagram hero AS-IS, max 3 brand logos
- REFERENCE AD STRUCTURE locked: left-text / right-diagram split, embedded accent pill in the headline, dark footer strip with three trust pills separated by thin dividers, circular tilted badge bottom-right, brand strip at the very bottom edge
- GLOBAL HARD RULES applied beneath those

GLOBAL HARD RULES
- 1080×1080 aspect ratio (square), nothing else
- Maintain ~60px safe-zone padding inside all four edges — no headlines, badges, CTAs, or critical text within this margin (brand strip may sit close to but not touching the bottom edge)
- All text readable at thumbnail size on mobile — every trust pill, every label, every region-header word clearly legible
- House diagram (IMAGE 3) used AS PROVIDED — do not redraw, do not regenerate ducting or airflow arrows, do not modify silhouette. Subtle palette/tint adjustment only
- Brand logos (IMAGES 4, 5, 6) used AS PROVIDED in native brand colours — do not recolour, do not redraw
- Maximum 3 brand logos (HR05)
- Do NOT include "0% interest" anywhere — "$0 Upfront Cost" is the only acceptable affordability phrase (HR07)
- Do NOT include any fixed-price installation amount — only the $57/week price (A1 archetype rule)
- Do NOT add any people, faces, or hands — no-people house-diagram variant
- Do NOT add review badges, star ratings, or Google logos to this ad
- Do NOT add urgency language ("limited time", "hurry", "ends soon") — savings-led calm authority, not urgency
- "DUCTED A/C" must appear prominently in BOTH the sub-line beneath the headline AND the price block — unmissable within one second (HR13)
- Headline must reference CUTTING bills (savings framing) — never "dreading", "shocked by", or any pain framing (that belongs to A4)
- Australian English throughout — "colour" not "color", "$" for AUD
- Pick a fresh modern palette per generation — do NOT default to the reference ad's grey/blue or the gold-standard's green
- Family Owned and Australian Owned trust badges generated fresh in the ad's palette per HR19, since both client booleans are true
- The ad should feel confident, premium-modern, and locally credible — if the output reads as cluttered, dated, or discount-retailer, it has failed

headline: CUT YOUR ENERGY BILLS BY UP TO 30%
prompt_text:
You are being given attached image(s):

IMAGE 1 — A reference ad for A1 Energy Bill Hero. Use this as a STYLE GUIDE only for density, hierarchy, and the "calm authority" feel. Do NOT copy its exact palette (light grey + sky blue), do NOT copy its exact layout (text-left / diagram-right), and do NOT copy its exact typography. The reference has "0% interest" in its bottom strip — that phrase is FORBIDDEN in your output (HR07). The reference also stacks three logos in its footer at a workable thumbnail size — match that footer thinking but pick fresh execution.

IMAGE 2 — Locked component: a 3D isometric house diagram in blue tones with white airflow ducting branching from a central unit out to ceiling vents across the roof. This is the HERO. Use AS-IS per HR02 — do not regenerate, do not modify the silhouette, ducting, fans, or proportions. You may apply a subtle palette tint for cohesion with your chosen background, but the diagram's structure must be preserved exactly. Place it as the dominant visual element occupying roughly the right 50–55% of the canvas, scaled large enough that the airflow story reads at thumbnail.

IMAGE 3, 4, 5 — Locked brand logos (Daikin, Mitsubishi Electric, Samsung). Use AS-IS — no recolouring, no redrawing, preserve official brand colours and proportions. Place all three on a footer strip along the bottom of the canvas, evenly spaced, at a size that is meaningfully readable on mobile (not decorative fine print).

---

Create a square 1080×1080 social media ad for a residential ducted air conditioning installer in Central Coast, Australia.

FUNNEL CONTEXT: This is a Cold/Warm top-of-funnel ad targeting Central Coast, Newcastle, and Hunter Valley homeowners scrolling Meta who are aware their power bills are climbing but haven't yet researched solutions. Its job is to lead with a calm, authoritative savings benefit and earn the click for a free on-site quote. Tone is trustworthy and benefit-led, NOT urgent or fear-based.

CRITICAL CLARITY RULE: The phrase "DUCTED A/C" (or "DUCTED AIR CONDITIONING") MUST appear prominently within the headline block or sub-headline so a scroller knows within 1 second exactly what is being sold. The chosen headline "CUT YOUR ENERGY BILLS BY UP TO 30%" does not name the product on its own — you MUST add a sub-line directly beneath the headline reading "WITH NEW DUCTED AIR CONDITIONING" (or equivalent phrasing such as "WITH A NEW DUCTED A/C SYSTEM") set in a smaller but still bold weight. This is non-negotiable.

PALETTE & MOOD: Calm, authoritative, savings-led. The reference ad leaned light-grey + sky-blue — pick a FRESH direction this generation. Lean toward a soft warm-cream or off-white background with a deep navy/charcoal as the primary type colour and a single accent colour (sage green, soft teal, or muted mustard work — pick one) reserved exclusively for the "30%" figure and one or two highlight elements. Avoid bargain-bin colour combinations. The mood should feel like a well-designed utility brochure or a premium energy-efficiency campaign — not a discount flyer. Pick fresh font pairings: a confident bold sans-serif for the headline and a clean medium-weight sans for supporting text.

LAYOUT (top to bottom, left to right):

1. TOP-LEFT HEADLINE BLOCK (occupying roughly the left 45% of the canvas, vertically centred in the upper two-thirds):
   - Small eyebrow tag: "SAVE ON YOUR POWER BILLS" set in the accent colour, uppercase, letter-spaced, roughly 22–26pt feel.
   - Main headline directly below, three lines stacked, oversized bold uppercase, deep navy/charcoal:
       Line 1: "CUT YOUR"
       Line 2: "ENERGY BILLS" (this line should be the largest — the visual anchor)
       Line 3: "BY UP TO" set in a smaller weight, with "30%" set as an oversized accent-colour numeral, either same line as "BY UP TO" or wrapped to a fourth line — whichever holds visual hierarchy best. The "30%" must dominate.
   - Sub-line beneath the headline block (CLARITY RULE): "WITH NEW DUCTED AIR CONDITIONING" — bold, navy/charcoal, sized clearly readable on mobile.

2. RIGHT 50–55% OF CANVAS — the locked house diagram (IMAGE 2) sits here as the hero. Scale large, vertically centred in the upper two-thirds, allowing the airflow ducting to "speak" the savings story visually. The diagram may bleed slightly off the right edge for a confident composition. Apply only the subtlest tint adjustment if needed for cohesion — preserve the existing blue tones and white airflow.

3. PRICE PILL — sitting in the lower-left quadrant, beneath the headline block:
   - "DUCTED A/C INSTALLED FROM" in small bold caps
   - "$57/WEEK" with "$57" as the dominant numeral and "/WEEK" smaller beside it. Use the accent colour or navy depending on which holds against the background.
   - Do NOT show a fixed-price anchor or struck-through RRP. A1 uses per-week price only.

4. VALUE STACK STRIP — a horizontal band sitting beneath the price pill and above the brand footer, set on a slightly darker tonal block (e.g. deep navy if the canvas is cream) for legibility. Three pills evenly spaced:
   - "Save up to 30% on bills"
   - "$1,299 in FREE Bonuses"
   - "$0 Upfront Cost"
   Each pill clearly readable on mobile — meaningful but secondary to the headline. Use a thin vertical divider between pills. Do NOT include any "0% interest" or finance phrasing.

5. CTA BUTTON — positioned bottom-right of the value stack strip OR as a clean pill within the strip: "BOOK A FREE ON-SITE QUOTE" in bold uppercase, accent-colour fill with navy/charcoal type, or inverse — whichever maximises contrast.

6. TRUST BADGE — small circular seal in the lower-right corner area: "5-YEAR GUARANTEE" in navy/charcoal on a cream/accent disc. Sized as a meaningful trust mark, not decorative. Do NOT make it compete with the headline.

7. CONDITIONAL TRUST BADGES (HR19) — client.family_owned is true and client.australian_owned is true. Add two small circular seal badges in the navy/cream/gold visual language: "Family Owned & Operated" and "Australian Owned." Place them as a small stacked pair in the lower-left corner OR as two accent pills along the bottom edge above the brand strip — whichever balances against the 5-Year Guarantee seal in the lower-right. Generate fresh in the ad's palette so they feel cohesive.

8. BRAND FOOTER STRIP — a clean horizontal strip along the bottom edge (sitting close to but not touching the 60px safe zone). The three locked brand logos (Daikin, Mitsubishi Electric, Samsung) evenly spaced, AS-IS in their official colours. Background of the strip should be a clean cream/white tone so all three logos read clearly without recolouring. Sized for mobile readability.

---

GLOBAL HARD RULES:

- Output 1080×1080 square. Maintain a 60px safe-zone of padding inside all four edges; no headline, badge, CTA, or critical text within this margin. The brand strip may sit close to but not touch the bottom edge.
- All text — including the value stack pills, price pill, trust badge, and brand strip — must be readable at thumbnail size on mobile. Nothing decorative-fine-print.
- The phrase "DUCTED AIR CONDITIONING" or "DUCTED A/C" MUST appear prominently — confirm it's visible within 1 second of viewing.
- Australian English. Use "$" for AUD.
- Maximum 3 brand logos. The three attached (Daikin, Mitsubishi Electric, Samsung) are the set — no others.
- Do NOT include "0% interest" or any finance phrasing. "$0 Upfront Cost" is the only acceptable affordability line.
- Do NOT generate AI faces or people. This ad has no people.
- Do NOT regenerate or redraw the house diagram. Use IMAGE 2 AS-IS, preserving silhouette, ducting, fans, vent placement, and proportions. Subtle palette tint for cohesion only.
- Do NOT recolour or redraw the brand logos. Use IMAGES 3, 4, 5 AS-IS in their official brand colours.
- Pick a fresh palette and fresh font pairings for this generation — do not default to the reference ad's light-grey/sky-blue look.
- Reference IMAGE 1 guides density, hierarchy, and the calm-authority feel only — do NOT copy its layout, palette, or specific text treatments.
- Trust badges (Family Owned, Australian Owned) generated fresh in this ad's palette to fit the design — navy/cream/gold seal language.
- No watermarks of any kind in the final output.
